'''Brad Drew''' (born 25 August 1975) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. A [[City New South Wales rugby league team|City New South Wales]] representative [[Rugby league positions#dummy half|hooker]], he played in Australia for [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]], [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]] and [[Canberra Raiders|Canberra]] and in England for [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] and [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]].

==Playing career==
Drew made his first grade debut for Penrith in round 19 1993 against [[Balmain Tigers|Balmain]] at [[Leichhardt Oval]].

Drew made no first grade appearances for three years before making his next appearances in 1996.  It wasn't later in his Penrith career that Drew featured more prominently for the team.  In 2000, Drew played for Penrith in their semi final defeat against Parramatta.  This was Drew's last game for Penrith and he signed for Parramatta in the aftermath.

Drew played at hooker for the [[Parramatta Eels]] in their [[2001 NRL grand final]] loss to the [[Newcastle Knights]].  In the 2002 season, Drew played for Parramatta in their finals loss against the [[Brisbane Broncos]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.parraeels.com.au/about/official-player-numbers/|title=Official Player Numbers|website=Parramatta Eels}}</ref>

In 2003, Drew signed for Canberra.  In the 2003 season, Canberra finished in 4th place on the table.  Drew played in their elimination finals loss against New Zealand.  The following year in 2004, Canberra again made the finals and Drew played in their finals defeat against the [[Sydney Roosters]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.raiders.com.au/about/all-time-first-grade-player-list/|title=Canberra Raiders Player Numbers: 1982–2019|website=Canberra Raiders}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://mobile.abc.net.au/news/2003-09-20/warriors-send-raiders-packing/1481518?pfm=sm/|title=Warriors send Raiders packing|publisher=ABC News|date=20 September 2003 }}</ref>

Drew later signed to play in the [[Super League]] for English club [[Huddersfield Giants]]. He played for Huddersfield in the [[2006 Challenge Cup]] Final at {{rlp|hk}} against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] but the Giants lost 12–42.

Drew was the fitness conditioner for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]], having been a critical part of the coaching and management staff for a number of years.<ref name="Management/Coaching Staff Profile">{{cite web|url=http://www.scotlandrl.com/index.php?option=com_content&view=article&id=60&Itemid=135|title=Management/Coaching Staff Profile|publisher=Scotland RL|date=2008-09-29|accessdate=2008-10-03|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080914230400/http://www.scotlandrl.com/index.php?option=com_content&view=article&id=60&Itemid=135|archivedate=14 September 2008}}</ref>

After an impressive stint at rivals [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], Drew re-signed for Huddersfield in September 2009.

==Coaching career==
In 2016, Drew became manager of [[Ron Massey Cup]] team the [[St Marys Saints]]. In his first year of management, Drew took St Marys to the Ron Massey Cup grand final but fell short to the [[Mount Pritchard Mounties]]. In 2017, St Marys finished as minor premiers in the Ron Massey Cup but were defeated by the [[Auburn Warriors]] in the preliminary final.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.stmarysstar.com.au/story/4862339/saints-coaches-sign-on-for-another-year/ |title=Saints coaches sign on for another year | St Marys-Mt Druitt Star |access-date=4 October 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171004085229/http://www.stmarysstar.com.au/story/4862339/saints-coaches-sign-on-for-another-year/ |archive-date=4 October 2017 |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.stmarysstar.com.au/story/3724736/saints-march-on/ |title=Saints march on | St Marys-Mt Druitt Star |access-date=4 October 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171004135710/http://www.stmarysstar.com.au/story/3724736/saints-march-on/ |archive-date=4 October 2017 |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://live.nswrl.com.au/comp/1986a31db4a830017?r=22d9e964bce1b2dfa |title=NSWRL Live – powered by Statedge |access-date=4 October 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171004135821/http://live.nswrl.com.au/comp/1986a31db4a830017?r=22d9e964bce1b2dfa |archive-date=4 October 2017 |url-status=dead }}</ref> Drew is set to return to St Marys as head coach of their Sydney Shield team for the 2026 season.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://westernweekender.com.au/2025/11/masked-panther-brad-drew-returns-to-saints/ |title=Masked Panther: Brad Drew returns to Saints; The Western Weekender |access-date=27 November 2025 }}</ref>

==After playing career==
In 2014, Drew became a Real Estate Agent in the Lower Blue Mountains region.
